examples.title: Character Frequency Counter
examples.description: Count frequency of each character in a string using HashMap
examples.code:
BEGIN
 FUNCTION countFrequency(String text)
 Map<Character, Integer> freq = NEW HashMap<>()
 
 FOR EACH char c IN text.toCharArray()
 // Method 1: getOrDefault
 Integer count = freq.getOrDefault(c, 0)
 freq.put(c, count + 1)
 
 // Alternative: computeIfAbsent
 // freq.computeIfAbsent(c, k -> 0)
 // freq.put(c, freq.get(c) + 1)
 END FOR
 
 RETURN freq
 END FUNCTION
 
 // Usage
 Map<Character, Integer> result = countFrequency("hello world")
 PRINT result
END
examples.explanation: Iterates through each character, using getOrDefault to get current count (or 0 if first occurrence), then increments and stores back. Results in {h=1, e=1, l=3, o=2, ' '=1, w=1, r=1, d=1}.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is string length
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is unique characters (up to 256 for ASCII)

examples.title: LRU Cache Implementation
examples.description: Implement Least Recently Used cache using LinkedHashMap
examples.code:
BEGIN
 CLASS LRUCache<K, V> EXTENDS LinkedHashMap<K, V>
 PRIVATE INTEGER maxCapacity
 
 CONSTRUCTOR(capacity)
 super(16, 0.75f, TRUE) // accessOrder = true
 this.maxCapacity = capacity
 END CONSTRUCTOR
 
 FUNCTION removeEldestEntry(Map.Entry<K, V> eldest)
 RETURN size() > maxCapacity
 END FUNCTION
 
 FUNCTION getOrDefault(key, defaultValue)
 RETURN super.getOrDefault(key, defaultValue)
 END FUNCTION
 
 FUNCTION put(key, value)
 RETURN super.put(key, value)
 END FUNCTION
 END CLASS
 
 // Usage
 LRUCache<String, Integer> cache = NEW LRUCache<>(3)
 cache.put("A", 1)
 cache.put("B", 2)
 cache.put("C", 3)
 cache.get("A") // A becomes most recently used
 cache.put("D", 4) // B is evicted (least recently used)
 PRINT cache // Output: {C=3, A=1, D=4}
END
examples.explanation: LinkedHashMap with accessOrder=true maintains entries in access order (most recently accessed at end). When size exceeds maxCapacity, removeEldestEntry() returns true, triggering eviction of eldest entry (first one).
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for get and put operations

examples.title: Word Occurrence Counter using merge()
examples.description: Count word occurrences efficiently using Map.merge()
examples.explanation: merge() is ideal for aggregation tasks. It atomically combines existing value with new value using the remapping function. computeIfAbsent() is useful for lazy initialization or expensive computation caching.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is unique words
